content:
POLICY DOCUMENT
===============
Policy Name: HospiCash Kenya
Policy Code: TUR-KE-HC-001
Country: Kenya
Product Type: HospiCash
Insurer: Turaco Insurance
Currency: KES (Kenyan Shilling) / USD equivalent shown in brackets

--------
HospiCash provides a daily cash benefit for every night a policyholder spends
as an inpatient in a registered hospital. The benefit is paid regardless of
actual medical bills, allowing the customer to cover lost income, transport,
or food for their family.

-----------
- Age: 18 to 65 years at enrollment
- Residency: Must be a Kenya resident
- Enrollment: Via approved Turaco partners (M-KOPA, Safaricom, Shell)
- Waiting period: 14 days from policy activation (no waiting period for accidents)

----------------
Daily Cash Benefit: KES 1,000 (~$7.50 USD) per night of inpatient stay
Maximum Benefit per Claim: KES 10,000 (~$75 USD) — 10 nights maximum
Maximum Benefit per Year: KES 20,000 (~$150 USD)
Minimum Qualifying Stay: 1 night (must be admitted, not just outpatient)

--------------------------------------
The following conditions qualify for HospiCash if treatment requires inpatient admission:
- Malaria (ICD-10: B50–B54)
- Typhoid Fever (ICD-10: A01.0)
- Pneumonia (ICD-10: J18)
- Tuberculosis (ICD-10: A15–A19)
- Acute Gastroenteritis / Diarrhoea (ICD-10: A09)
- Urinary Tract Infection (ICD-10: N39.0)
- Hypertensive Crisis (ICD-10: I10)
- Diabetic Emergency (ICD-10: E11)
- Appendicitis (ICD-10: K37)
- Road Traffic Accident injuries (ICD-10: V01–V99)
- Maternity complications (ICD-10: O00–O99) — female policyholders only
- Surgical procedures requiring overnight admission

----------
- Outpatient visits (no overnight stay)
- Cosmetic or elective procedures not medically necessary
- Pre-existing conditions diagnosed before enrollment (first 6 months)
- Self-inflicted injuries or substance abuse
- Treatment outside Kenya
- Experimental treatments not approved by Kenya Medical Board

------------------
To file a claim, the following documents must be submitted via WhatsApp:
1. Hospital discharge summary or admission receipt
2. Receipt showing total charges and itemized bill
3. Diagnosis note from attending doctor (must include ICD-10 code or diagnosis name)
4. Patient's Turaco policy number
5. National ID or passport copy of policyholder

------
- Approved claims paid to M-PESA within 3 working days
- Amount = (Number of qualifying nights) x KES 1,000
- Capped at KES 10,000 per single claim event

--------------------
Single claim maximum: KES 10,000 (10 nights)
Annual maximum: KES 20,000
Per-night benefit: KES 1,000
Minimum nights for claim: 1

CONTACT
-------
Claims WhatsApp: +254 700 000 000
Email: claims@turaco.com
Operating Hours: Monday–Friday 8am–6pm EAT

POLICY DOCUMENT
===============
Policy Name: Last Expense Kenya
Policy Code: TUR-KE-LE-001
Country: Kenya
Product Type: Last Expense (Funeral Cash Benefit)
Insurer: Turaco Insurance
Currency: KES (Kenyan Shilling) / USD equivalent shown in brackets

--------
Last Expense insurance provides a lump-sum cash payment to the beneficiary
upon the death of the policyholder. The benefit is intended to cover funeral
and burial costs, reducing financial burden on the family during bereavement.

-----------
- Age: 18 to 70 years at enrollment
- Residency: Must be a Kenya resident
- Enrollment: Via approved Turaco partners
- Waiting period: 6 months for natural causes; no waiting period for accidental death

----------------
Death Benefit (Natural Causes): KES 30,000 (~$225 USD)
Death Benefit (Accidental Death): KES 50,000 (~$375 USD)
Repatriation Assistance: Up to KES 10,000 (~$75 USD) for transport of remains within Kenya

------------------------
- Natural illness (after 6-month waiting period)
- Road traffic accident
- Workplace accident
- Drowning
- Fire-related death
- Any sudden, unexpected cause

----------
- Suicide or self-inflicted death
- Death resulting from participation in criminal activity
- Death from war or civil unrest
- Death while under the influence of non-prescribed substances

------------------
To file a Last Expense claim, the following must be submitted via WhatsApp:
1. Certified copy of Death Certificate (from registrar of births and deaths)
2. Burial permit
3. National ID of the deceased
4. National ID of the beneficiary / claimant
5. Proof of relationship to the deceased (if beneficiary is not spouse)
6. Turaco policy number of the deceased

------
- Paid to nominated beneficiary via M-PESA or bank transfer within 3 working days
- Lump sum, not per-night benefit

--------------------
Natural death benefit: KES 30,000
Accidental death benefit: KES 50,000
Repatriation support: KES 10,000
Claim period: Must be filed within 90 days of death

CONTACT
-------
Claims WhatsApp: +254 700 000 000
Email: claims@turaco.com
Operating Hours: Monday–Friday 8am–6pm EAT

POLICY DOCUMENT
===============
Policy Name: HospiCash Nigeria
Policy Code: TUR-NG-HC-001
Country: Nigeria
Product Type: HospiCash
Insurer: Turaco Insurance
Currency: NGN (Nigerian Naira) / USD equivalent shown in brackets

--------
HospiCash Nigeria provides a daily cash benefit for inpatient hospital stays.
Designed for low-income earners across Lagos, Abuja, Kano, Port Harcourt, and
other major Nigerian cities, distributed through NIBSS Instant Pay and partner
mobile wallets.

-----------
- Age: 18 to 65 years at enrollment
- Residency: Must be a Nigeria resident
- Enrollment: Via approved Turaco Nigeria partners
- Waiting period: 14 days from activation (no waiting period for accidents)

----------------
Daily Cash Benefit: NGN 5,000 (~$6 USD) per night of inpatient stay
Maximum Benefit per Claim: NGN 50,000 (~$60 USD) — 10 nights maximum
Maximum Benefit per Year: NGN 100,000 (~$120 USD)
Minimum Qualifying Stay: 1 night (formal inpatient admission required)

--------------------------------------
- Malaria (ICD-10: B50–B54)
- Typhoid Fever (ICD-10: A01.0)
- Pneumonia (ICD-10: J18)
- Tuberculosis (ICD-10: A15–A19)
- Sickle Cell Crisis (ICD-10: D57) — high prevalence in Nigeria
- Meningitis (ICD-10: G03)
- Hypertensive Emergency (ICD-10: I10)
- Diabetic Emergency (ICD-10: E11)
- Acute Gastroenteritis (ICD-10: A09)
- Urinary Tract Infection requiring admission (ICD-10: N39.0)
- Road Traffic Accident injuries (ICD-10: V01–V99)
- Maternity complications (ICD-10: O00–O99) — female policyholders only
- Surgical procedures requiring overnight admission

----------
- Outpatient consultations without overnight admission
- Cosmetic procedures
- Pre-existing conditions (first 6 months)
- Self-inflicted injuries
- Treatment outside Nigeria
- Traditional / herbal medicine costs

------------------
Submit via WhatsApp:
1. Hospital discharge summary or inpatient admission receipt
2. Itemized hospital bill
3. Doctor's note with diagnosis (ICD-10 code or diagnosis name)
4. Policyholder's Turaco policy number
5. Valid ID (NIN, Driver's Licence, International Passport, or Voter's Card)

------
- Approved claims paid to OPay, Kuda Bank, or bank account within 3 working days
- Amount = (qualifying nights) x NGN 5,000
- Capped at NGN 50,000 per single claim event

--------------------
Single claim maximum: NGN 50,000 (10 nights)
Annual maximum: NGN 100,000
Per-night benefit: NGN 5,000
Minimum nights: 1

CONTACT
-------
Claims WhatsApp: +234 700 000 0000
Email: claims-ng@turaco.com
Operating Hours: Monday–Friday 8am–6pm WAT

POLICY DOCUMENT
===============
Policy Name: Last Expense Nigeria
Policy Code: TUR-NG-LE-001
Country: Nigeria
Product Type: Last Expense (Funeral Cash Benefit)
Insurer: Turaco Insurance
Currency: NGN (Nigerian Naira) / USD equivalent shown in brackets

--------
Last Expense Nigeria provides a lump-sum cash payment to the designated
beneficiary upon death of the policyholder. The payment helps families manage
the high cost of Nigerian burial ceremonies and funeral arrangements.

-----------
- Age: 18 to 70 years at enrollment
- Residency: Must be a Nigeria resident
- Enrollment: Via approved Turaco Nigeria partners
- Waiting period: 6 months for natural causes; no waiting period for accidents

----------------
Death Benefit (Natural Causes): NGN 100,000 (~$120 USD)
Death Benefit (Accidental Death): NGN 150,000 (~$180 USD)
Repatriation Assistance: Up to NGN 30,000 (~$36 USD) for transport within Nigeria

------------------------
- Natural illness (post 6-month waiting period)
- Road traffic accident
- Workplace accident
- Drowning
- Fire-related death
- Any sudden, unexpected accidental cause

----------
- Suicide or self-inflicted death
- Death from participation in criminal activity
- Death from war or terrorism
- Death from substance abuse

------------------
Submit via WhatsApp:
1. Certified Death Certificate issued by Nigerian authorities
2. Burial or cremation permit
3. NIN (National Identification Number) or valid ID of deceased
4. Valid ID of beneficiary/claimant
5. Evidence of relationship to deceased if beneficiary is not spouse
6. Turaco policy number of the deceased

------
- Paid to nominated beneficiary via OPay, bank transfer, or mobile wallet
- Must be filed within 90 days of death
- Processed within 3 working days of complete document submission

--------------------
Natural death benefit: NGN 100,000
Accidental death benefit: NGN 150,000
Repatriation support: NGN 30,000
Filing deadline: 90 days from date of death

CONTACT
-------
Claims WhatsApp: +234 700 000 0000
Email: claims-ng@turaco.com
Operating Hours: Monday–Friday 8am–6pm WAT

POLICY DOCUMENT
===============
Policy Name: HospiCash Uganda
Policy Code: TUR-UG-HC-001
Country: Uganda
Product Type: HospiCash
Insurer: Turaco Insurance
Currency: UGX (Ugandan Shilling) / USD equivalent shown in brackets

--------
HospiCash Uganda provides a daily cash benefit for each night spent as an
inpatient in a registered Ugandan hospital. Distributed via Airtel Money and
Turaco partner networks across Uganda.

-----------
- Age: 18 to 65 years at enrollment
- Residency: Must be a Uganda resident
- Enrollment: Via Airtel Money Uganda, Tugende, or other approved partners
- Waiting period: 14 days from activation (no waiting period for accidents)

----------------
Daily Cash Benefit: UGX 30,000 (~$8 USD) per night of inpatient stay
Maximum Benefit per Claim: UGX 300,000 (~$80 USD) — 10 nights maximum
Maximum Benefit per Year: UGX 600,000 (~$160 USD)
Minimum Qualifying Stay: 1 night (inpatient admission required)

--------------------------------------
- Malaria (ICD-10: B50–B54) — high prevalence, automatically fast-tracked
- Typhoid Fever (ICD-10: A01.0)
- Pneumonia (ICD-10: J18)
- Tuberculosis (ICD-10: A15–A19)
- Cholera (ICD-10: A00) — specific to Uganda endemic zones
- Acute Gastroenteritis (ICD-10: A09)
- Sickle Cell Crisis (ICD-10: D57)
- Urinary Tract Infection (ICD-10: N39.0)
- Road Traffic Accident injuries (ICD-10: V01–V99)
- Boda-boda (motorcycle) accident injuries
- Maternity complications (ICD-10: O00–O99) — female policyholders only
- Surgical procedures requiring overnight admission

----------
- Outpatient visits without formal admission
- Cosmetic or elective procedures
- Pre-existing conditions (first 6 months)
- Self-inflicted injuries
- Treatment outside Uganda
- Traditional medicine costs

------------------
Submit via WhatsApp:
1. Hospital discharge summary or inpatient receipt
2. Itemized bill from hospital
3. Doctor's diagnosis note (ICD-10 code or diagnosis name required)
4. Policyholder's Turaco policy number
5. National ID (Uganda National ID or valid passport)

------
- Approved claims paid to Airtel Money or MTN Mobile Money within 3 working days
- Amount = (qualifying nights) x UGX 30,000
- Capped at UGX 300,000 per claim

--------------------
Single claim maximum: UGX 300,000 (10 nights)
Annual maximum: UGX 600,000
Per-night benefit: UGX 30,000
Minimum nights: 1

CONTACT
-------
Claims WhatsApp: +256 700 000 000
Email: claims-ug@turaco.com
Operating Hours: Monday–Friday 8am–6pm EAT

POLICY DOCUMENT
===============
Policy Name: Personal Accident Uganda
Policy Code: TUR-UG-PA-001
Country: Uganda
Product Type: Personal Accident
Insurer: Turaco Insurance
Currency: UGX (Ugandan Shilling) / USD equivalent shown in brackets

--------
Personal Accident insurance provides cash compensation for accidental injuries,
permanent disability, or accidental death. Designed for boda-boda riders,
construction workers, and informal sector workers in Uganda.

-----------
- Age: 18 to 60 years at enrollment
- Residency: Must be a Uganda resident
- Enrollment: Via Tugende, Airtel Money, or approved Turaco partners
- Waiting period: None — coverage begins immediately upon activation

----------------
Accidental Death Benefit: UGX 1,000,000 (~$270 USD)
Permanent Total Disability: UGX 1,000,000 (~$270 USD)
Permanent Partial Disability: UGX 100,000–500,000 depending on severity
Medical Expense Reimbursement (Accident-related): Up to UGX 200,000 (~$54 USD)
Hospital Cash (Accident-related admission): UGX 30,000/night up to 10 nights

-----------------------
- Road traffic accidents (boda-boda, matatu, vehicle)
- Workplace accidents (construction, factory, farm)
- Falls resulting in fractures or injuries
- Electrocution accidents
- Burns from fire or explosion
- Drowning (non-suicidal)
- Any sudden, violent, external event causing bodily injury

----------------
Loss of both hands or feet: 100% (UGX 1,000,000)
Loss of one hand and one foot: 100% (UGX 1,000,000)
Loss of sight in both eyes: 100% (UGX 1,000,000)
Loss of one leg above knee: 60% (UGX 600,000)
Loss of one arm above elbow: 60% (UGX 600,000)
Loss of one eye: 40% (UGX 400,000)
Loss of thumb: 20% (UGX 200,000)
Loss of index finger: 10% (UGX 100,000)

----------
- Self-inflicted injuries or suicide attempts
- Injuries from participation in criminal activities
- Injuries while under the influence of alcohol or drugs
- Pre-existing physical disabilities
- War, civil unrest, or terrorism
- Extreme sports (mountaineering, skydiving) unless declared at enrollment
- Illness-related conditions (use HospiCash for illness)

------------------
Submit via WhatsApp:
1. Police report (for road accidents — P3 form)
2. Hospital medical report describing injuries and treatment
3. Discharge summary
4. Photos of injuries if available (optional but helpful)
5. National ID of claimant
6. Turaco policy number

------
- Medical reimbursements: within 3 working days
- Disability claims: within 5 working days after assessment
- Death claims: within 5 working days of receiving all documents
- All payments via Airtel Money or MTN Mobile Money

--------------------
Accidental death: UGX 1,000,000
Total disability: UGX 1,000,000
Medical reimbursement cap: UGX 200,000
Hospital cash per night: UGX 30,000
Maximum hospital cash per claim: UGX 300,000 (10 nights)

CONTACT
-------
Claims WhatsApp: +256 700 000 000
Email: claims-ug@turaco.com
Operating Hours: Monday–Friday 8am–6pm EAT
